title: Fermented Velvety Slovakian Lemon Bake
description: A creamy, sweet, and tangy dessert featuring unwaxed lemon and a hint of spices
instructions: In a small saucepan, heat 1 tablespoon of safflower oil over low heat.
In a medium bowl, whisk together almond flour, yeast, and salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, sugar, vanilla extract, and ground cinnamon.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
In a separate saucepan, whisk together 2 tablespoons of cornstarch and 3 tablespoons of water over low heat, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the batter and mix until well combined.
Pour the batter into a greased 9-inch round cake pan.
Gently tap the pan to remove any air bubbles and brush the surface with unsalted butter.
Bake for 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
This recipe is best served warm, topped with a dollop of whipped cream or a sprinkle of powdered sugar, if desired.
Note: This recipe uses active dry yeast to create a light and airy texture, but you can also use 1 1/2 teaspoons of baking powder as a substitute if you prefer a quicker method.
